title: Quiz: Band Theory in Depth
description: Test your understanding of band theory concepts

questions.question: In the tight binding model, what parameter determines the band width?
questions.explanation: The band width W = 4t (in 1D) or W = 2zt (z = coordination number). The hopping integral t measures overlap between neighboring orbitals and directly determines how much the energy varies with k.

questions.question: In the nearly free electron model, what causes band gaps to open?

options.text: The periodic potential causing standing waves at zone boundaries

questions.explanation: At zone boundaries (k = G/2), the nearly free electron model predicts standing waves that interact with the periodic potential. The cos(Gx/2) wave has lower energy (on atoms) and sin(Gx/2) has higher energy (between atoms), creating the gap.

questions.question: What is the effective mass at a band minimum?
questions.explanation: The effective mass is defined as m* = ℏ²/(∂²E/∂k²). At a band minimum, ∂²E/∂k² > 0, giving a positive mass. The curvature of the band determines how electrons respond to external fields.

questions.question: Why can't silicon make efficient LEDs?

options.text: Its band gap is too large
It has an indirect band gap

questions.explanation: Silicon has an indirect band gap - the conduction band minimum is not at the same k as the valence band maximum. Optical transitions require phonon assistance, making emission weak and inefficient for LEDs.

questions.question: DFT with standard functionals typically overestimates semiconductor band gaps.
questions.explanation: False. DFT with standard functionals (LDA, GGA) typically underestimates band gaps. For example, Si shows ~0.5 eV vs experimental 1.1 eV. This is due to the band gap problem in Kohn-Sham DFT.

questions.question: Which materials have direct band gaps? (Select all)
questions.explanation: GaAs and GaN have direct band gaps and are used for LEDs and lasers. Silicon and Germanium have indirect band gaps with their conduction band minima away from the Γ point.
